reasoning:
Based on the resolution criteria and background information, calculating ten times the 30-day average plus one over the past 360 days means the required number of Riots in the 30 days prior to the resolution is 11 or more (since the most recent reference value is exactly 1.000, and 10 x 1.000 = 10, meaning "more than 10"). For the first resolution date (Feb 22, 2026), the 30-day window is roughly Jan 23 to Feb 22. For the second date (Mar 17, 2026), the window is roughly Feb 15 to Mar 17. 
Searches for recent events in Sao Tome and Principe in English and Portuguese return no reports of ongoing riots, protests, or civil unrest in early 2026 (the only recent news being routine updates on upcoming elections and diplomatic items). Sao Tome and Principe is historically very stable; ACLED recorded 0 'Riots' over the past 360 days. The likelihood that 11 separate 'Riot' events have occurred or will suddenly occur in the 30-day windows (one of which is almost over) with no current media coverage is astronomically low. Both probabilities are set to the lowest allowed reasonable floor (0.02) to reflect this high confidence.
